strategy_notes: STOP iteration pipeline. Q6 has a critical semantic correctness bug: the atomic-based aggregation (lines 162-164) fails under work-stealing morsel execution. Only one thread per thread_id slot successfully updates, causing accumulated values from other threads to be lost. The fix is deterministic: replace atomic aggregation with simple thread-local partitioning (each thread accumulates into partial_results[thread_id] without synchronization), then merge at end. This is a blocking correctness issue—no performance optimization is meaningful until this is fixed. Recommend: (1) Apply critical_fixes[0] to restore correctness, (2) Validate result against benchmark, (3) Restart optimization cycle with a clean baseline.
notes: Iteration 1 regressed due to architectural flaw in parallel aggregation. The atomic CAS pattern is unsuitable for accumulating work from multiple threads into per-thread slots under work-stealing. Standard solution: thread-local accumulation + final merge (no atomics during scan phase). Once correctness is restored and query produces correct result (≈1.23 trillion), re-enter optimization loop to target DuckDB's 19.98ms baseline from a correct foundation.
